content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

July 18, 2005

Kelly C. Quinn
Law Offices of Mark J. Werksman
801 South Figueroa Street, 11th Floor
Los Angeles, CA 90017

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-05-126a

Dear Ms. Quinn:

This letter is in regard to your follow-up request for advice regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹

In a letter to you dated June 21, 2005, staff formally requested that you provide the name of the candidate referred to in your letter (as specified in regulation 18329(b)(2)(A)), as well as state whether the questions in your letter were hypothetical in nature or related to past conduct. When you failed to respond to this letter, we closed the letter file number (W-05-126). We informed you of this in our letter of July 7, 2005.

Under the provisions of California Code of Regulations 18329(b)(8)(B), unless you identify the candidate and are writing as his or her authorized representative, the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide analysis. The Commission declines to provide advice on hypothetical inquiries. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(D), enclosed.)

¹ Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997 of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. W-05-126a
Page 2

If you are able to obtain the requested information (identity of the
candidate) in the future, please feel free to submit a new inquiry. Your most
recent letter, received July 11, 2005, merely restates your question and is not
sufficient to initiate a new advice request.

Should you have any questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel
Legal Division

JW:rd
